title: Bridging peptide presentation and T cell recognition with multi-task learning
data_sources: Peptide-MHC binding benchmarks (Jurtz et al. 2017 lineage)
Peptide-TCR binding benchmarks (Wells et al. 2020 lineage)

data_scope.hla_coverage: pan-HLA via NetMHCpan-4.0 pseudo-sequences; exact allele count not stated in public metadata
data_scope.sequence_counts: not stated in public metadata (Brief Communication format)
data_scope.input_modality: amino acid sequences only (no 3D structure required at inference)

core_conclusion: UnifyImmun, a transformer-based model with alternating multi-task learning and cross-attention, jointly predicts peptide-HLA and peptide-TCR binding by sharing a continuously updated peptide encoder, directly capturing the coupled nature of antigen immunogenicity that prior single-task models missed.
why_it_matters: First joint model for pMHC and pTCR binding, removing compound chaining error; shared encoder encodes the biological HLA-TCR co-dependency; advances neoantigen prioritization for personalized cancer vaccines; demonstrates alternating multi-task learning as a general architectural principle.
why_nature: Genuine architectural novelty at the deep learning-biology interface; high-impact biomedical application in cancer immunotherapy; mechanistically justified transformer application; tightly scoped Brief Communication with clear ablative contribution.
added_value_to_my_research: Meta-learning extension for rare HLA alleles -> few-shot spatial transferability to data-sparse urban regions in MGWR/2SFCA models
Protein LM initialization for encoders -> fine-tuning geospatial foundation models (SatCLIP/GeoCLIP) for downstream spatial prediction tasks
Auxiliary conformational flexibility task -> physics-informed auxiliary constraints (autocorrelation/flow conservation) regularizing GNN accessibility models
Multimodal MS immunopeptidomics fusion -> cross-modal attention fusion for GPS + social media + remote sensing in urban health prediction
Alternating multi-task cross-attention design -> coupled spatial health outcome modeling (accessibility + health outcome jointly) replacing separate pipelines

related.title: Meta-learning for T cell receptor binding specificity and beyond
related.relation: meta-learning extension for few-shot pTCR binding prediction; directly extends UnifyImmun's TCR task to rare alleles

related.title: Learning functional properties of proteins with language models
related.relation: protein language model pretraining to initialize UnifyImmun's sequence encoders with evolutionary priors

related.title: Learning conformational flexibility of immune receptors
related.relation: adds conformational dynamics as a third auxiliary task in UnifyImmun's multi-task framework

related.title: A large-scale unified deep learning model for peptide mass spectrum interpretation trained on multimodal data
related.relation: multimodal MS immunopeptidomics fusion as a fourth cross-attention stream in UnifyImmun

related.title: A multi-task learning strategy to pretrain models for medical image analysis
related.relation: general multi-task pretraining design applicable to UnifyImmun's alternating update strategy in other biomedical domains

figures.caption: UnifyImmun alternating multi-task learning workflow: (a) neoantigen presentation cartoon, (b) TCR 6-11-p53R175H-HLA-A*02:01 structure (PDB 7RM4), (c) input sequence formats, (d) full model architecture with three self-attention encoders, two cross-attention layers, and shared iteratively updated peptide encoder
